title: gilia congesta subsp. iberidifolia
intro: Gilia congesta subsp. iberidifolia, also known as Yellow toadflax, is a perennial plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. Gilia congesta subsp. iberidifolia is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
